architecture.summary: A constrained decoding system for discrete diffusion language models that samples from the mean-field posterior restricted to constraints expressible as finite automata. At each denoising step, the system performs exact inference over a chain-structured graphical model induced by the automaton, reweighting the model's factorized mean-field distribution with transition and emission factors derived from the constraint graph. Information flows from the dLLM's per-position token probabilities through forward-backward message passing and either sequential ancestral sampling or a novel log-depth tree sampler that exposes parallelism on GPUs.

biblio.title: Constrained Decoding for Diffusion Language Models via Efficient Inference over Finite Automata

claims.statement: The approach guarantees constraint satisfaction by construction, supports both greedy and sampling-based decoding, and is compatible with parallel and block-wise decoding under arbitrary remasking schedules.
On BFCL-Live, the approach improves Dream-7B's greedy decoding accuracy from 63.9% to 71.5%
On BFCL-Live, stochastic sampling accuracy improves from 22.3% to 69.0%, where the unconstrained baseline collapses
The method incurs under 5% wall-clock overhead relative to unconstrained decoding on BFCL
Log-depth tree sampling reduces sequential depth from O(n) to O(log n) while preserving the same sampling distribution
On Sudoku, the method improves Dream-7B's greedy accuracy from 36.4% to 92.4%
The method remains robust as the number of denoising steps decreases from 256 to 16, whereas the unconstrained baseline deteriorates sharply
The method focuses on finite automata and does not cover richer constraints such as context-free grammars
